title: Lush Argentinian Ume Stew
description: A fragrant, velvety stew that blends fermented ume with Asian miso and Argentine herbs, delivering a gentle energy boost while staying shojin-friendly.
instructions: Soak the dried ume plum in 500ml of cold water overnight to soften and release its flavors.
In a pot, combine the soaked ume, kombu, and a pinch of salt, and bring to a gentle simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the kombu and stir in the miso paste until fully dissolved.
Add the soy milk, garlic, ginger, and sweet potato cubes, and simmer for another 15 minutes until the potatoes are tender.
Stir in the spinach and cook for 2 minutes until the leaves wilt.
Taste and adjust seasoning with a small pinch of salt if needed.
Garnish with chopped green onion before serving.
Serve hot, accompanied by a side of quinoa or crusty bread if desired.
Enjoy the herbal, velvety texture and gentle energizing lift of this shojin-friendly stew.
